articles.headline: India's Green Hydrogen Mission Marks Significant Progress, Drives Clean Energy Transition
articles.explainer:
• The National Green Hydrogen Mission, launched in 2023, has achieved critical milestones in its phased implementation strategy.
• New incentives for green hydrogen production and electrolyser manufacturing have been announced to boost domestic capacity.
• India aims to become a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, leveraging its renewable energy potential.
• The mission targets a production capacity of 5 MMT (million tonnes) per annum by 2030, reducing dependence on fossil fuel imports.
• Collaborative projects with industry and research institutions are accelerating R&D in cost-effective green hydrogen technologies.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Investment models
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Target production capacity: 5 MMT per annum by 2030.
Aim to reduce fossil fuel imports and achieve energy independence.
Incentives for electrolyser manufacturing are part of the mission.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy security and climate commitments.
Analyze the economic potential and challenges in establishing India as a global green hydrogen hub.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private sector investment in scaling up green hydrogen production.
Examine the environmental benefits and technological advancements required for widespread adoption of green hydrogen.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting green hydrogen production facilities with renewable energy sources.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Sudanese Student Fatally Stabbed in Punjab University Campus, Raising Campus Safety Concerns
articles.explainer:
• Mohamed Wada, a Sudanese student at Lovely Professional University (LPU), was fatally stabbed after intervening in an alleged harassment incident.
• Six other students have been arrested in connection with the incident, which occurred on the university campus.
• The tragedy has sparked widespread concern regarding the safety and security of students, particularly international students, on Indian university campuses.
• Student organizations and international student bodies have called for stricter security measures and better grievance redressal mechanisms.
• The incident highlights broader issues of campus violence, cultural integration challenges, and the need for robust institutional support for foreign students.
gsSubTopics: Social empowerment
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: Incident occurred at Lovely Professional University (LPU) in Punjab.
Victim was a Sudanese national.
The event led to arrests of six other students.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to violence on university campuses in India and suggest comprehensive preventative measures.
Discuss the challenges faced by international students in India and how universities and the government can ensure their safety and well-being.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current campus security protocols and recommend improvements for a safer academic environment.
Examine the societal implications of such incidents on India's image as a global education destination.
articles.imageDescription: A generic image of a university campus with students.
articles.trivia: India is one of the top destinations for international students, particularly from African and South Asian countries.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Right to Privacy in Landmark Data Protection Ruling
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a significant judgment upholding stringent data protection standards, emphasizing the fundamental right to privacy.
• The ruling came in a case challenging the collection and use of personal data by private entities without explicit consent.
• The Court reiterated the principles established in the Puttaswamy judgment, affirming privacy as an intrinsic part of Article 21.
• It directed the government to expedite the enactment of a comprehensive data protection law, aligning with global best practices.
• The verdict is expected to have far-reaching implications for digital governance, e-commerce, and the tech industry in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Right to Privacy is a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.
Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared privacy as a fundamental right.
Supreme Court urged for a comprehensive data protection law.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of the right to privacy in India and its implications for digital rights and governance.
Discuss the challenges in balancing individual privacy with national security and economic development in the digital age.
Evaluate the potential impact of a robust data protection law on various sectors, including technology, finance, and healthcare.
Suggest measures to ensure effective implementation and enforcement of data protection regulations in India.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel and legal books, symbolizing judicial pronouncements.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment was delivered by a nine-judge bench, one of the largest in Supreme Court history.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Dialogue Reinforces Maritime Security and Regional Cooperation in Indo-Pacific
articles.explainer:
• India and ASEAN nations concluded a high-level dialogue on maritime security, focusing on shared challenges in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions emphasized cooperation in counter-piracy, disaster relief, search and rescue operations, and freedom of navigation.
• The dialogue underscored India's Act East Policy and its commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• Participants stressed the importance of adherence to international law, particularly UNCLOS, for regional stability.
• New initiatives for joint maritime exercises and intelligence sharing were proposed to enhance operational synergy.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: ASEAN is a regional intergovernmental organization of ten Southeast Asian countries.
India's Act East Policy focuses on enhancing engagement with Southeast Asian nations.
UNCLOS (United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea) provides a framework for maritime activities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of maritime security cooperation between India and ASEAN for stability in the Indo-Pacific region.
Analyze how India's Act East Policy contributes to its strategic interests and regional influence.
Evaluate the challenges posed by geopolitical rivalries and non-traditional threats to maritime security in the region.
Propose measures for strengthening India-ASEAN collaboration in areas beyond traditional security, such as blue economy and connectivity.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships conducting a joint exercise in the ocean.
articles.trivia: ASEAN was founded on August 8, 1967, in Bangkok, Thailand, with five original member states.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its latest review meeting.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach, balancing inflation management with supporting economic growth.
• While retail inflation has shown signs of easing, global uncertainties continue to pose risks to the domestic economic outlook.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to withdrawing accommodation gradually to ensure inflation aligns with the target.
• Analysts anticipate continued stability in interest rates, providing predictability for businesses and investors.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which commercial banks borrow money from the RBI.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the policy interest rates.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping growth in mind.
Inflation target for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and effectiveness of the RBI's monetary policy in managing inflation and fostering economic growth in India.
Analyze the various factors that influence the Monetary Policy Committee's decisions on interest rates.
Evaluate the impact of global economic trends and geopolitical events on India's monetary policy stance.
Examine the challenges faced by the RBI in achieving its dual mandate of price stability and growth support.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India logo with financial graphs in the background.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Prototype
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) conducted a successful test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype, 'Pushpak'.
• The test involved a precise landing experiment, demonstrating autonomous navigation and control capabilities.
• This achievement is a crucial step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, significantly reducing space access costs.
• RLV technology aims to make space missions more affordable and sustainable, enhancing India's competitive edge in the global space market.
• Future tests will focus on orbital re-entry and hypersonic flight capabilities.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV prototype is named 'Pushpak'.
RLV technology aims to reduce the cost of space launches.
The test focused on autonomous navigation and precise landing.
ISRO is developing a two-stage orbital reusable launch vehicle.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its economic implications.
Analyze how ISRO's advancements in space technology contribute to national development and international collaboration.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities associated with developing and deploying reusable launch systems.
Examine the role of indigenous technology development in achieving self-reliance in the space sector.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's RLV 'Pushpak' descending for landing.
articles.trivia: The concept of reusable launch vehicles dates back to the 1960s, but only a few nations have successfully developed operational systems.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Progress in Skill Development
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation noted significant progress in integrating skill development.
• Vocational education has been introduced at various levels, aiming to enhance employability and reduce the skill gap.
• The review highlighted improved access to digital learning resources and teacher training programs.
• Challenges remain in infrastructure development and ensuring equitable access to quality education in remote areas.
• Stakeholders emphasized the need for continued collaboration between government, academia, and industry to fully realize NEP's objectives.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system.
Focus on integrating vocational education and skill development.
Digital learning resources are a key component of NEP implementation.
The policy seeks to universalize education from preschool to secondary level.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key reforms introduced by NEP 2020 and their potential impact on India's human resource development.
Discuss the challenges in implementing NEP 2020, particularly concerning infrastructure, funding, and teacher training.
Evaluate the effectiveness of vocational education integration in bridging the skill gap and enhancing youth employability.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to quality education across all regions and socio-economic strata under NEP.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaged in a vocational training session.
articles.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the first education policy in India in 34 years, replacing the 1986 policy.

articles.headline: New Report Highlights Intensifying Threats to Western Ghats Biodiversity, Calls for Urgent Action
articles.explainer:
• A recent environmental report indicates a critical decline in biodiversity within the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
• Key threats identified include habitat fragmentation, climate change impacts, invasive species, and unsustainable resource extraction.
• The report emphasizes the loss of endemic flora and fauna, crucial for the ecological balance of the region.
• It calls for stronger policy implementation, increased protected areas, and active community participation in conservation efforts.
• Recommendations include sustainable tourism models and stricter enforcement against illegal mining and deforestation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Western Ghats are one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
Known for high levels of endemism in flora and fauna.
Threats include climate change, habitat loss, and invasive species.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the major threats it faces.
Analyze the effectiveness of current conservation policies and suggest measures for strengthening biodiversity protection in the region.
Evaluate the role of local communities and indigenous knowledge in sustainable resource management and conservation.
Examine the challenges in balancing developmental needs with environmental preservation in ecologically sensitive areas like the Western Ghats.
articles.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with dense forests.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and influence the Indian monsoon weather pattern.

articles.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Garners Global Praise at G20 Tech Summit
articles.explainer:
• India's pioneering Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, including Aadhaar, UPI, and CoWIN, received widespread acclaim at a recent G20 Tech Summit.
• International delegates lauded the model for its ability to drive financial inclusion, deliver public services efficiently, and foster innovation.
• India presented its 'Stack' approach as a scalable and interoperable solution for other developing nations.
• Discussions focused on replicating India's success in building open, secure, and inclusive digital ecosystems.
• The recognition strengthens India's position as a leader in digital transformation and a proponent of technology for development.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: India's DPI includes Aadhaar, UPI, and CoWIN.
DPI aims to provide public services and financial inclusion.
The model was showcased at a G20 Tech Summit.
It is seen as a scalable solution for developing nations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key components and benefits of India's Digital Public Infrastructure model for governance and economic development.
Analyze how DPI has contributed to financial inclusion and efficient public service delivery in India.
Evaluate the potential for replicating India's DPI model in other developing countries and the challenges involved.
Examine the role of DPI in fostering innovation and digital transformation while addressing concerns of data privacy and security.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing interconnected digital services and people.
articles.trivia: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) recorded over 13 billion transactions in a single month in late 2024.

articles.headline: Border Infrastructure Development Accelerated in Northeast, Enhancing Connectivity and Security
articles.explainer:
• The Union government has significantly ramped up infrastructure development projects along India's northeastern borders.
• These projects include new roads, bridges, tunnels, and railway lines, aiming to improve connectivity to remote areas.
• The initiative seeks to bolster national security, facilitate trade with neighboring countries, and promote economic development in the region.
• Focus is also on dual-use infrastructure that serves both civilian and strategic defense purposes.
• The enhanced connectivity is expected to integrate the Northeast more closely with the national mainstream and boost tourism.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
prelimsPoints: Northeast India shares borders with several countries like Bangladesh, Myanmar, China, and Bhutan.
Infrastructure development in border areas is crucial for national security and economic integration.
Projects include roads, bridges, tunnels, and railway lines.
The initiative supports the Act East Policy by improving trade linkages.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of accelerating border infrastructure development in India's Northeast region.
Analyze the socio-economic impact of improved connectivity on the lives of people in remote border areas.
Evaluate the challenges involved in infrastructure development in the challenging terrain of the Northeast and suggest sustainable solutions.
Examine how enhanced border infrastructure contributes to national security and India's Act East Policy objectives.
articles.imageDescription: A newly constructed bridge spanning a river in a mountainous region of Northeast India.
articles.trivia: The Bogibeel Bridge in Assam, the longest rail-cum-road bridge in India, is a key infrastructure project in the Northeast.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms in Disaster Management Act, Focus on Climate Resilience
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted a report recommending significant amendments to the Disaster Management Act, 2005.
• The recommendations advocate for a greater focus on climate change adaptation, mitigation, and building long-term resilience.
• The panel stressed the need for proactive disaster preparedness rather than reactive response mechanisms.
• It suggested strengthening local self-governments and empowering communities in disaster risk reduction strategies.
• The report also called for enhanced funding, technology integration, and inter-agency coordination for effective disaster management.
gsSubTopics: Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Disaster Management Act was enacted in 2005.
National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) is the apex body for disaster management in India.
Recommendations include focusing on climate change adaptation and proactive preparedness.
Emphasis on strengthening local self-governments.
mainsPoints: Analyze the effectiveness of the Disaster Management Act, 2005, in addressing contemporary disaster challenges, including climate-induced events.
Discuss the importance of shifting from a reactive to a proactive and resilience-focused approach in disaster management.
Evaluate the role of various stakeholders, from national to local levels, in implementing effective disaster risk reduction strategies.
Suggest specific amendments to the Disaster Management Act that can enhance India's climate resilience and preparedness.
articles.imageDescription: A rescue team working in a disaster-affected area.
articles.trivia: India is highly vulnerable to natural disasters, with over 58% of its landmass prone to earthquakes and 12% to floods.

articles.headline: India's Agricultural Exports Witness Sustained Growth, Boosting Farmers' Income
articles.explainer:
• India has recorded consistent growth in its agricultural and processed food exports over the past year, contributing to increased farmer incomes.
• Key export commodities include rice, spices, fruits, vegetables, and marine products, diversifying India's export basket.
• Government initiatives like the 'Agriculture Export Policy' and 'One District One Product' scheme have facilitated this growth.
• Challenges remain in improving supply chain infrastructure, cold storage facilities, and adherence to international quality standards.
• The growth in exports is crucial for achieving the target of doubling farmers' income and strengthening India's position in global food trade.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: APEDA (Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority) promotes agricultural exports.
The 'One District One Product' (ODOP) scheme aims to promote indigenous products.
India exports a variety of agricultural products including rice, spices, and marine products.
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors contributing to the growth in India's agricultural exports and its impact on farmer welfare.
Analyze the challenges faced by Indian agricultural exporters in meeting international quality standards and logistics.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies and schemes in promoting agricultural exports and value addition.
Suggest measures to further enhance agricultural export potential and integrate Indian farmers into global value chains.
articles.imageDescription: A vibrant image of various fresh fruits and vegetables, symbolizing agricultural produce.
articles.trivia: India is the world's largest producer of milk, pulses, and spices.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey Uncovers Ancient Harappan Settlement in Gujarat, Sheds New Light on Indus Valley Civilization
articles.explainer:
• Recent archaeological excavations in Gujarat have unearthed a previously unknown Harappan settlement, providing fresh insights into the Indus Valley Civilization.
• The site reveals intricate urban planning, pottery, tools, and evidence of trade networks, indicating a well-developed society.
• The discovery helps in understanding the geographical spread and regional variations of the Harappan civilization.
• Preliminary findings suggest a potential link between this site and other major Harappan centers like Dholavira and Lothal.
• Further research is expected to shed light on the economic activities, social structure, and eventual decline of the settlement.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: Indus Valley Civilization is also known as Harappan Civilization.
Gujarat hosts several important Harappan sites like Dholavira and Lothal.
Archaeological discoveries provide insights into ancient urban planning, trade, and culture.
Dholavira is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of new archaeological discoveries in understanding the nuances and expanse of the Indus Valley Civilization.
Analyze the key features of Harappan urban planning, economic activities, and social structure based on available evidence.
Evaluate the challenges faced by archaeologists in excavating and preserving ancient sites in India.
Examine how such discoveries contribute to our understanding of India's ancient cultural heritage and its global connections.
articles.imageDescription: An archaeological excavation site showing ancient pottery and structural remains.
articles.trivia: The Indus Valley Civilization was one of the three early civilizations of the Near East and South Asia, along with Ancient Egypt and Mesopotamia.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Heal in India' Portal to Boost Medical Tourism and Healthcare Exports
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched the 'Heal in India' portal, a dedicated platform to promote medical tourism in the country.
• The portal provides comprehensive information on accredited healthcare facilities, treatment packages, and visa assistance for international patients.
• It aims to position India as a global hub for affordable and quality medical treatment, attracting patients from across the world.
• The initiative is part of a broader strategy to boost healthcare exports and generate foreign exchange earnings.
• It also seeks to standardize services and ensure a seamless experience for medical tourists, enhancing India's soft power.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The 'Heal in India' portal is for promoting medical tourism.
It provides information on healthcare facilities and visa assistance.
Aims to make India a global hub for medical treatment.
Part of efforts to boost healthcare exports.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of medical tourism in India to generate revenue, create employment, and enhance India's global image.
Analyze the challenges faced by India's medical tourism sector, including infrastructure, quality control, and regulatory frameworks.
Evaluate the role of government initiatives like 'Heal in India' in standardizing services and attracting international patients.
Suggest measures to further strengthen India's position as a preferred destination for medical value travel.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized logo of 'Heal in India' with a global map and medical symbols.
articles.trivia: India is known for offering high-quality medical treatments at a fraction of the cost compared to Western countries.

articles.headline: Rise in Cyber Attacks on Critical Infrastructure Prompts Call for Enhanced National Cybersecurity Strategy
articles.explainer:
• India has witnessed a concerning increase in sophisticated cyber attacks targeting critical infrastructure sectors, including power grids, financial systems, and telecommunications.
• These attacks, often state-sponsored or from organized cybercrime groups, pose significant national security and economic risks.
• Experts are calling for an urgent review and enhancement of India's National Cybersecurity Strategy and incident response mechanisms.
• Key recommendations include strengthening public-private partnerships, investing in advanced cyber defense technologies, and building a skilled cybersecurity workforce.
• The government is reportedly considering new legislative measures to mandate higher security standards for critical infrastructure operators.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Critical Infrastructure includes power grids, financial systems, and telecommunications.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the nodal agency for cybersecurity incidents.
Cyber attacks pose threats to national security and economy.
Need for stronger public-private partnerships in cybersecurity.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cyber threats to India's critical infrastructure and their potential impact on national security and economic stability.
Analyze the strengths and weaknesses of India's current cybersecurity framework and strategy in protecting vital digital assets.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships and international cooperation in building a resilient cybersecurity ecosystem.
Suggest comprehensive measures, including legislative, technological, and human resource development, to enhance India's cybersecurity capabilities.
articles.imageDescription: A digital representation of interconnected networks with a lock icon, symbolizing cybersecurity.
articles.trivia: India ranks among the top countries targeted by cyberattacks globally.

articles.headline: India Pledges Significant Support for Global South Climate Adaptation Fund at UN Forum
articles.explainer:
• India announced a substantial financial contribution and technical assistance pledge to a newly established Global South Climate Adaptation Fund at a UN forum.
• The fund aims to support developing nations in enhancing their resilience to climate change impacts, particularly in vulnerable regions.
• India emphasized the principle of Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) in climate finance.
• The move reinforces India's leadership role among developing countries on climate action and its commitment to South-South cooperation.
• Discussions also focused on technology transfer and capacity building to help developing nations implement adaptation strategies effectively.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Global South refers to developing countries.
CBDR-RC (Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities) is a principle in international climate agreements.
The fund focuses on climate adaptation for vulnerable nations.
India plays a leadership role among developing countries on climate action.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's pledge to the Global South Climate Adaptation Fund in the context of international climate justice and South-South cooperation.
Analyze the concept of Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) and its relevance in global climate negotiations.
Evaluate the challenges faced by developing nations in adapting to climate change and the role of international finance and technology transfer.
Examine India's evolving role as a leader in climate action among developing countries and its implications for global environmental governance.
articles.imageDescription: A world map highlighting the Global South nations, with symbols of climate action.
articles.trivia: The term 'Global South' typically refers to countries in Africa, Latin America, and developing Asia.

articles.headline: India Aims for Accelerated Solar Energy Capacity Expansion, Boosts Manufacturing
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced ambitious targets for solar energy capacity expansion, aiming for 500 GW by 2030.
• New policies and incentives are being rolled out to boost domestic manufacturing of solar cells, modules, and related equipment.
• The focus is on reducing reliance on imports, fostering self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat) in the solar sector, and creating jobs.
• Challenges include land acquisition, grid integration, and ensuring stable financing for large-scale projects.
• The expansion is critical for achieving India's climate goals and ensuring energy security in the long run.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: India's target for renewable energy capacity is 500 GW by 2030.
Focus on domestic manufacturing of solar cells and modules.
Atmanirbhar Bharat initiative promotes self-reliance.
Ministry of New and Renewable Energy oversees these initiatives.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's ambitious targets for solar energy expansion and their significance for climate action and energy security.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in boosting domestic manufacturing of solar equipment under the Atmanirbhar Bharat initiative.
Evaluate the economic and environmental benefits of transitioning to a greater share of renewable energy in India's power mix.
Suggest policy interventions and technological advancements required to overcome barriers to large-scale solar energy deployment.
articles.imageDescription: A vast solar power plant with numerous solar panels under a clear sky.
articles.trivia: India's Bhadla Solar Park in Rajasthan is one of the largest solar parks in the world.

articles.headline: Smart Cities Mission Shows Progress in Urban Transformation, Focus on Sustainability
articles.explainer:
• The Smart Cities Mission has achieved significant milestones in urban infrastructure development and technology integration across selected cities.
• Key areas of progress include smart mobility solutions, waste management, water supply, and digital governance platforms.
• The mission emphasizes sustainable urban planning, green building initiatives, and citizen-centric services.
• Challenges persist in project implementation delays, funding utilization, and ensuring equitable development for all urban residents.
• Future focus is on leveraging data analytics for better urban management and replicating successful models in more cities.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: Smart Cities Mission launched in 2015.
Aims to promote cities that provide core infrastructure and give a decent quality of life.
Focuses on smart solutions for mobility, waste management, and water supply.
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs is the nodal ministry.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and achievements of the Smart Cities Mission in transforming India's urban landscape.
Analyze the challenges encountered in the implementation of Smart Cities projects, including funding, governance, and citizen participation.
Evaluate the extent to which the mission has contributed to sustainable urban development and improved quality of life.
Suggest measures to overcome implementation bottlenecks and ensure more inclusive and equitable smart city development.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic city skyline with green spaces and integrated technology.
articles.trivia: The Smart Cities Mission aims to develop 100 cities across India.

highlights: India's Green Hydrogen Mission achieves critical milestones, aiming for 5 MMT production by 2030.
Sudanese student fatally stabbed in Punjab university, prompting calls for enhanced campus safety and international student welfare.
Supreme Court reinforces Right to Privacy in a landmark data protection ruling, urging comprehensive legislation.
ISRO successfully tests 'Pushpak' RLV prototype, a crucial step towards reducing space access costs.
RBI maintains status quo on repo rate, balancing inflation management with economic growth support.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. It was launched in 2023 with a target to achieve 5 MMT (million tonnes) per annum production capacity by 2030.
2. The mission primarily focuses on the export of green hydrogen and does not include incentives for domestic electrolyser manufacturing.
3. It aims to reduce India's dependence on fossil fuel imports.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The National Green Hydrogen Mission was launched in 2023 with a target of 5 MMT per annum production capacity by 2030. Statement 2 is incorrect. The mission includes incentives for electrolyser manufacturing to boost domestic capacity. Statement 3 is correct. A key aim of the mission is to reduce dependence on fossil fuel imports and achieve energy independence.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements best describes the significance of the Supreme Court's recent ruling on data protection and privacy?
options: (a) It has for the first time recognized privacy as a fundamental right under the Indian Constitution.
(b) It reiterated the principles of the Puttaswamy judgment, affirming privacy as intrinsic to Article 21, and urged for a comprehensive data protection law.
(c) It mandated that all private entities must share user data with the government for national security purposes without explicit consent.
(d) It overturned previous judgments regarding data collection, stating that only the government can collect personal data.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) It reiterated the principles of the Puttaswamy judgment, affirming privacy as intrinsic to Article 21, and urged for a comprehensive data protection law.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court's Puttaswamy judgment in 2017 already declared privacy as a fundamental right. The recent ruling reiterated these principles and called for expedited data protection legislation, not for the first time recognizing privacy. Options (c) and (d) are contrary to the spirit of data protection.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following about India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI):
1. It includes platforms like Aadhaar, UPI, and CoWIN.
2. It has been lauded globally for driving financial inclusion and efficient public service delivery.
3. The model is primarily designed for developed nations and is not easily replicable in developing countries.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. India's DPI includes Aadhaar, UPI, and CoWIN. Statement 2 is correct. The model has received global praise for its success in financial inclusion and public services. Statement 3 is incorrect. The model is seen as a scalable and interoperable solution for other developing nations, not primarily for developed ones, and is considered easily replicable.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a primary objective of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020?
options: (a) Integrating vocational education at various levels.
(b) Enhancing employability and reducing the skill gap.
(c) Centralizing all higher education institutions under a single regulatory body.
(d) Improving access to digital learning resources.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Centralizing all higher education institutions under a single regulatory body.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The NEP 2020 aims for a 'light but tight' regulatory framework with multiple bodies, not a single centralized one, to foster autonomy while maintaining standards. Options (a), (b), and (d) are indeed key objectives of the NEP 2020.

prelimsMocks.question:
With respect to the Western Ghats, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. They are older than the Himalayas.
2. They are recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
3. They have a significant influence on the Indian monsoon weather pattern.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The Western Ghats are indeed geologically older than the Himalayas. They are one of the world's biodiversity hotspots with high endemism. Their geographical position and orientation play a crucial role in influencing the Indian monsoon.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the strategic importance of India's Green Hydrogen Mission for achieving energy security and climate commitments. Discuss the challenges and opportunities in establishing India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Environmental pollution and degradation; Conservation; Science and Technology- developments.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Green Hydrogen Mission and its goals. Discuss its strategic importance for energy independence, reducing emissions, and economic growth. Analyze opportunities like renewable energy potential, job creation, and export markets. Detail challenges such as technology costs, infrastructure development, water availability, and policy support. Conclude with the roadmap for India to become a global hub.

mainsMocks.question: The recent incident of violence against an international student on a university campus highlights broader issues of campus safety and welfare. Discuss the challenges faced by international students in India and suggest comprehensive measures that universities and the government can undertake to ensure a safer and more inclusive academic environment.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Social empowerment; Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources. GS2: Government policies and interventions.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by acknowledging the specific incident and the general context of campus safety. Elaborate on challenges faced by international students (cultural integration, language barriers, discrimination, safety concerns, grievance redressal). Propose comprehensive measures including stricter security, improved counseling, cultural sensitization programs, robust grievance mechanisms, clear policies, and community engagement for both universities and the government.

mainsMocks.question: India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model has garnered global attention. Discuss the key components and benefits of this model for governance and economic development within India. Evaluate its potential for replication in other developing countries, highlighting associated challenges.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development; E-governance; Important International institutions. GS3: Indian Economy; Science and Technology.
mainsMocks.approach: Define DPI and list its key components (e.g., Aadhaar, UPI, CoWIN). Explain benefits such as financial inclusion, efficient public service delivery, innovation, and transparency. Discuss the potential for replication in other developing countries, citing examples or theoretical advantages. Address challenges like data privacy, digital divide, infrastructure readiness, political will, and interoperability issues in different contexts.

quickBytes.text: The National Green Hydrogen Mission targets 5 MMT production capacity by 2030.
Right to Privacy is a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.
ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype is named 'Pushpak'.
The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and a biodiversity hotspot.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the policy interest rates.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 is the first education policy in 34 years.
Aadhaar, UPI, and CoWIN are key components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
The Disaster Management Act was enacted in 2005, with NDMA as the apex body.
The 'Heal in India' portal aims to boost medical tourism and healthcare exports.
CBDR-RC (Common But Differentiated Responsibilities) is a principle in climate agreements.

GS1.headline: Sudanese Student Fatally Stabbed in Punjab University Campus, Raising Campus Safety Concerns
GS1.explainer:
• Mohamed Wada, a Sudanese student at Lovely Professional University (LPU), was fatally stabbed after intervening in an alleged harassment incident.
• Six other students have been arrested in connection with the incident, which occurred on the university campus.
• The tragedy has sparked widespread concern regarding the safety and security of students, particularly international students, on Indian university campuses.
• Student organizations and international student bodies have called for stricter security measures and better grievance redressal mechanisms.
• The incident highlights broader issues of campus violence, cultural integration challenges, and the need for robust institutional support for foreign students.
gsSubTopics: Social empowerment
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: Incident occurred at Lovely Professional University (LPU) in Punjab.
Victim was a Sudanese national.
The event led to arrests of six other students.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to violence on university campuses in India and suggest comprehensive preventative measures.
Discuss the challenges faced by international students in India and how universities and the government can ensure their safety and well-being.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current campus security protocols and recommend improvements for a safer academic environment.
Examine the societal implications of such incidents on India's image as a global education destination.
GS1.imageDescription: A generic image of a university campus with students.
GS1.trivia: India is one of the top destinations for international students, particularly from African and South Asian countries.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey Uncovers Ancient Harappan Settlement in Gujarat, Sheds New Light on Indus Valley Civilization
GS1.explainer:
• Recent archaeological excavations in Gujarat have unearthed a previously unknown Harappan settlement, providing fresh insights into the Indus Valley Civilization.
• The site reveals intricate urban planning, pottery, tools, and evidence of trade networks, indicating a well-developed society.
• The discovery helps in understanding the geographical spread and regional variations of the Harappan civilization.
• Preliminary findings suggest a potential link between this site and other major Harappan centers like Dholavira and Lothal.
• Further research is expected to shed light on the economic activities, social structure, and eventual decline of the settlement.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: Indus Valley Civilization is also known as Harappan Civilization.
Gujarat hosts several important Harappan sites like Dholavira and Lothal.
Archaeological discoveries provide insights into ancient urban planning, trade, and culture.
Dholavira is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of new archaeological discoveries in understanding the nuances and expanse of the Indus Valley Civilization.
Analyze the key features of Harappan urban planning, economic activities, and social structure based on available evidence.
Evaluate the challenges faced by archaeologists in excavating and preserving ancient sites in India.
Examine how such discoveries contribute to our understanding of India's ancient cultural heritage and its global connections.
GS1.imageDescription: An archaeological excavation site showing ancient pottery and structural remains.
GS1.trivia: The Indus Valley Civilization was one of the three early civilizations of the Near East and South Asia, along with Ancient Egypt and Mesopotamia.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Right to Privacy in Landmark Data Protection Ruling
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a significant judgment upholding stringent data protection standards, emphasizing the fundamental right to privacy.
• The ruling came in a case challenging the collection and use of personal data by private entities without explicit consent.
• The Court reiterated the principles established in the Puttaswamy judgment, affirming privacy as an intrinsic part of Article 21.
• It directed the government to expedite the enactment of a comprehensive data protection law, aligning with global best practices.
• The verdict is expected to have far-reaching implications for digital governance, e-commerce, and the tech industry in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Right to Privacy is a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.
Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared privacy as a fundamental right.
Supreme Court urged for a comprehensive data protection law.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of the right to privacy in India and its implications for digital rights and governance.
Discuss the challenges in balancing individual privacy with national security and economic development in the digital age.
Evaluate the potential impact of a robust data protection law on various sectors, including technology, finance, and healthcare.
Suggest measures to ensure effective implementation and enforcement of data protection regulations in India.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel and legal books, symbolizing judicial pronouncements.
GS2.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment was delivered by a nine-judge bench, one of the largest in Supreme Court history.

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Dialogue Reinforces Maritime Security and Regional Cooperation in Indo-Pacific
GS2.explainer:
• India and ASEAN nations concluded a high-level dialogue on maritime security, focusing on shared challenges in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions emphasized cooperation in counter-piracy, disaster relief, search and rescue operations, and freedom of navigation.
• The dialogue underscored India's Act East Policy and its commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• Participants stressed the importance of adherence to international law, particularly UNCLOS, for regional stability.
• New initiatives for joint maritime exercises and intelligence sharing were proposed to enhance operational synergy.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: ASEAN is a regional intergovernmental organization of ten Southeast Asian countries.
India's Act East Policy focuses on enhancing engagement with Southeast Asian nations.
UNCLOS (United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea) provides a framework for maritime activities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of maritime security cooperation between India and ASEAN for stability in the Indo-Pacific region.
Analyze how India's Act East Policy contributes to its strategic interests and regional influence.
Evaluate the challenges posed by geopolitical rivalries and non-traditional threats to maritime security in the region.
Propose measures for strengthening India-ASEAN collaboration in areas beyond traditional security, such as blue economy and connectivity.
GS2.imageDescription: Naval ships conducting a joint exercise in the ocean.
GS2.trivia: ASEAN was founded on August 8, 1967, in Bangkok, Thailand, with five original member states.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Progress in Skill Development
GS2.explainer:
• A comprehensive review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation noted significant progress in integrating skill development.
• Vocational education has been introduced at various levels, aiming to enhance employability and reduce the skill gap.
• The review highlighted improved access to digital learning resources and teacher training programs.
• Challenges remain in infrastructure development and ensuring equitable access to quality education in remote areas.
• Stakeholders emphasized the need for continued collaboration between government, academia, and industry to fully realize NEP's objectives.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system.
Focus on integrating vocational education and skill development.
Digital learning resources are a key component of NEP implementation.
The policy seeks to universalize education from preschool to secondary level.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key reforms introduced by NEP 2020 and their potential impact on India's human resource development.
Discuss the challenges in implementing NEP 2020, particularly concerning infrastructure, funding, and teacher training.
Evaluate the effectiveness of vocational education integration in bridging the skill gap and enhancing youth employability.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to quality education across all regions and socio-economic strata under NEP.
GS2.imageDescription: Students engaged in a vocational training session.
GS2.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the first education policy in India in 34 years, replacing the 1986 policy.

GS2.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Garners Global Praise at G20 Tech Summit
GS2.explainer:
• India's pioneering Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, including Aadhaar, UPI, and CoWIN, received widespread acclaim at a recent G20 Tech Summit.
• International delegates lauded the model for its ability to drive financial inclusion, deliver public services efficiently, and foster innovation.
• India presented its 'Stack' approach as a scalable and interoperable solution for other developing nations.
• Discussions focused on replicating India's success in building open, secure, and inclusive digital ecosystems.
• The recognition strengthens India's position as a leader in digital transformation and a proponent of technology for development.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: India's DPI includes Aadhaar, UPI, and CoWIN.
DPI aims to provide public services and financial inclusion.
The model was showcased at a G20 Tech Summit.
It is seen as a scalable solution for developing nations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key components and benefits of India's Digital Public Infrastructure model for governance and economic development.
Analyze how DPI has contributed to financial inclusion and efficient public service delivery in India.
Evaluate the potential for replicating India's DPI model in other developing countries and the challenges involved.
Examine the role of DPI in fostering innovation and digital transformation while addressing concerns of data privacy and security.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing interconnected digital services and people.
GS2.trivia: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) recorded over 13 billion transactions in a single month in late 2024.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms in Disaster Management Act, Focus on Climate Resilience
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted a report recommending significant amendments to the Disaster Management Act, 2005.
• The recommendations advocate for a greater focus on climate change adaptation, mitigation, and building long-term resilience.
• The panel stressed the need for proactive disaster preparedness rather than reactive response mechanisms.
• It suggested strengthening local self-governments and empowering communities in disaster risk reduction strategies.
• The report also called for enhanced funding, technology integration, and inter-agency coordination for effective disaster management.
gsSubTopics: Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Disaster Management Act was enacted in 2005.
National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) is the apex body for disaster management in India.
Recommendations include focusing on climate change adaptation and proactive preparedness.
Emphasis on strengthening local self-governments.
mainsPoints: Analyze the effectiveness of the Disaster Management Act, 2005, in addressing contemporary disaster challenges, including climate-induced events.
Discuss the importance of shifting from a reactive to a proactive and resilience-focused approach in disaster management.
Evaluate the role of various stakeholders, from national to local levels, in implementing effective disaster risk reduction strategies.
Suggest specific amendments to the Disaster Management Act that can enhance India's climate resilience and preparedness.
GS2.imageDescription: A rescue team working in a disaster-affected area.
GS2.trivia: India is highly vulnerable to natural disasters, with over 58% of its landmass prone to earthquakes and 12% to floods.

GS2.headline: Government Launches 'Heal in India' Portal to Boost Medical Tourism and Healthcare Exports
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched the 'Heal in India' portal, a dedicated platform to promote medical tourism in the country.
• The portal provides comprehensive information on accredited healthcare facilities, treatment packages, and visa assistance for international patients.
• It aims to position India as a global hub for affordable and quality medical treatment, attracting patients from across the world.
• The initiative is part of a broader strategy to boost healthcare exports and generate foreign exchange earnings.
• It also seeks to standardize services and ensure a seamless experience for medical tourists, enhancing India's soft power.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The 'Heal in India' portal is for promoting medical tourism.
It provides information on healthcare facilities and visa assistance.
Aims to make India a global hub for medical treatment.
Part of efforts to boost healthcare exports.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of medical tourism in India to generate revenue, create employment, and enhance India's global image.
Analyze the challenges faced by India's medical tourism sector, including infrastructure, quality control, and regulatory frameworks.
Evaluate the role of government initiatives like 'Heal in India' in standardizing services and attracting international patients.
Suggest measures to further strengthen India's position as a preferred destination for medical value travel.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized logo of 'Heal in India' with a global map and medical symbols.
GS2.trivia: India is known for offering high-quality medical treatments at a fraction of the cost compared to Western countries.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Significant Support for Global South Climate Adaptation Fund at UN Forum
GS2.explainer:
• India announced a substantial financial contribution and technical assistance pledge to a newly established Global South Climate Adaptation Fund at a UN forum.
• The fund aims to support developing nations in enhancing their resilience to climate change impacts, particularly in vulnerable regions.
• India emphasized the principle of Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) in climate finance.
• The move reinforces India's leadership role among developing countries on climate action and its commitment to South-South cooperation.
• Discussions also focused on technology transfer and capacity building to help developing nations implement adaptation strategies effectively.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Global South refers to developing countries.
CBDR-RC (Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities) is a principle in international climate agreements.
The fund focuses on climate adaptation for vulnerable nations.
India plays a leadership role among developing countries on climate action.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's pledge to the Global South Climate Adaptation Fund in the context of international climate justice and South-South cooperation.
Analyze the concept of Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) and its relevance in global climate negotiations.
Evaluate the challenges faced by developing nations in adapting to climate change and the role of international finance and technology transfer.
Examine India's evolving role as a leader in climate action among developing countries and its implications for global environmental governance.
GS2.imageDescription: A world map highlighting the Global South nations, with symbols of climate action.
GS2.trivia: The term 'Global South' typically refers to countries in Africa, Latin America, and developing Asia.

GS2.headline: Smart Cities Mission Shows Progress in Urban Transformation, Focus on Sustainability
GS2.explainer:
• The Smart Cities Mission has achieved significant milestones in urban infrastructure development and technology integration across selected cities.
• Key areas of progress include smart mobility solutions, waste management, water supply, and digital governance platforms.
• The mission emphasizes sustainable urban planning, green building initiatives, and citizen-centric services.
• Challenges persist in project implementation delays, funding utilization, and ensuring equitable development for all urban residents.
• Future focus is on leveraging data analytics for better urban management and replicating successful models in more cities.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: Smart Cities Mission launched in 2015.
Aims to promote cities that provide core infrastructure and give a decent quality of life.
Focuses on smart solutions for mobility, waste management, and water supply.
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs is the nodal ministry.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and achievements of the Smart Cities Mission in transforming India's urban landscape.
Analyze the challenges encountered in the implementation of Smart Cities projects, including funding, governance, and citizen participation.
Evaluate the extent to which the mission has contributed to sustainable urban development and improved quality of life.
Suggest measures to overcome implementation bottlenecks and ensure more inclusive and equitable smart city development.
GS2.imageDescription: A futuristic city skyline with green spaces and integrated technology.
GS2.trivia: The Smart Cities Mission aims to develop 100 cities across India.

GS3.headline: India's Green Hydrogen Mission Marks Significant Progress, Drives Clean Energy Transition
GS3.explainer:
• The National Green Hydrogen Mission, launched in 2023, has achieved critical milestones in its phased implementation strategy.
• New incentives for green hydrogen production and electrolyser manufacturing have been announced to boost domestic capacity.
• India aims to become a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, leveraging its renewable energy potential.
• The mission targets a production capacity of 5 MMT (million tonnes) per annum by 2030, reducing dependence on fossil fuel imports.
• Collaborative projects with industry and research institutions are accelerating R&D in cost-effective green hydrogen technologies.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Investment models
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Target production capacity: 5 MMT per annum by 2030.
Aim to reduce fossil fuel imports and achieve energy independence.
Incentives for electrolyser manufacturing are part of the mission.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy security and climate commitments.
Analyze the economic potential and challenges in establishing India as a global green hydrogen hub.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private sector investment in scaling up green hydrogen production.
Examine the environmental benefits and technological advancements required for widespread adoption of green hydrogen.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting green hydrogen production facilities with renewable energy sources.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its latest review meeting.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach, balancing inflation management with supporting economic growth.
• While retail inflation has shown signs of easing, global uncertainties continue to pose risks to the domestic economic outlook.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to withdrawing accommodation gradually to ensure inflation aligns with the target.
• Analysts anticipate continued stability in interest rates, providing predictability for businesses and investors.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which commercial banks borrow money from the RBI.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the policy interest rates.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping growth in mind.
Inflation target for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and effectiveness of the RBI's monetary policy in managing inflation and fostering economic growth in India.
Analyze the various factors that influence the Monetary Policy Committee's decisions on interest rates.
Evaluate the impact of global economic trends and geopolitical events on India's monetary policy stance.
Examine the challenges faced by the RBI in achieving its dual mandate of price stability and growth support.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India logo with financial graphs in the background.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Prototype
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) conducted a successful test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype, 'Pushpak'.
• The test involved a precise landing experiment, demonstrating autonomous navigation and control capabilities.
• This achievement is a crucial step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, significantly reducing space access costs.
• RLV technology aims to make space missions more affordable and sustainable, enhancing India's competitive edge in the global space market.
• Future tests will focus on orbital re-entry and hypersonic flight capabilities.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV prototype is named 'Pushpak'.
RLV technology aims to reduce the cost of space launches.
The test focused on autonomous navigation and precise landing.
ISRO is developing a two-stage orbital reusable launch vehicle.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its economic implications.
Analyze how ISRO's advancements in space technology contribute to national development and international collaboration.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities associated with developing and deploying reusable launch systems.
Examine the role of indigenous technology development in achieving self-reliance in the space sector.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's RLV 'Pushpak' descending for landing.
GS3.trivia: The concept of reusable launch vehicles dates back to the 1960s, but only a few nations have successfully developed operational systems.

GS3.headline: New Report Highlights Intensifying Threats to Western Ghats Biodiversity, Calls for Urgent Action
GS3.explainer:
• A recent environmental report indicates a critical decline in biodiversity within the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
• Key threats identified include habitat fragmentation, climate change impacts, invasive species, and unsustainable resource extraction.
• The report emphasizes the loss of endemic flora and fauna, crucial for the ecological balance of the region.
• It calls for stronger policy implementation, increased protected areas, and active community participation in conservation efforts.
• Recommendations include sustainable tourism models and stricter enforcement against illegal mining and deforestation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Western Ghats are one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
Known for high levels of endemism in flora and fauna.
Threats include climate change, habitat loss, and invasive species.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the major threats it faces.
Analyze the effectiveness of current conservation policies and suggest measures for strengthening biodiversity protection in the region.
Evaluate the role of local communities and indigenous knowledge in sustainable resource management and conservation.
Examine the challenges in balancing developmental needs with environmental preservation in ecologically sensitive areas like the Western Ghats.
GS3.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with dense forests.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and influence the Indian monsoon weather pattern.

GS3.headline: Border Infrastructure Development Accelerated in Northeast, Enhancing Connectivity and Security
GS3.explainer:
• The Union government has significantly ramped up infrastructure development projects along India's northeastern borders.
• These projects include new roads, bridges, tunnels, and railway lines, aiming to improve connectivity to remote areas.
• The initiative seeks to bolster national security, facilitate trade with neighboring countries, and promote economic development in the region.
• Focus is also on dual-use infrastructure that serves both civilian and strategic defense purposes.
• The enhanced connectivity is expected to integrate the Northeast more closely with the national mainstream and boost tourism.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
prelimsPoints: Northeast India shares borders with several countries like Bangladesh, Myanmar, China, and Bhutan.
Infrastructure development in border areas is crucial for national security and economic integration.
Projects include roads, bridges, tunnels, and railway lines.
The initiative supports the Act East Policy by improving trade linkages.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of accelerating border infrastructure development in India's Northeast region.
Analyze the socio-economic impact of improved connectivity on the lives of people in remote border areas.
Evaluate the challenges involved in infrastructure development in the challenging terrain of the Northeast and suggest sustainable solutions.
Examine how enhanced border infrastructure contributes to national security and India's Act East Policy objectives.
GS3.imageDescription: A newly constructed bridge spanning a river in a mountainous region of Northeast India.
GS3.trivia: The Bogibeel Bridge in Assam, the longest rail-cum-road bridge in India, is a key infrastructure project in the Northeast.

GS3.headline: India's Agricultural Exports Witness Sustained Growth, Boosting Farmers' Income
GS3.explainer:
• India has recorded consistent growth in its agricultural and processed food exports over the past year, contributing to increased farmer incomes.
• Key export commodities include rice, spices, fruits, vegetables, and marine products, diversifying India's export basket.
• Government initiatives like the 'Agriculture Export Policy' and 'One District One Product' scheme have facilitated this growth.
• Challenges remain in improving supply chain infrastructure, cold storage facilities, and adherence to international quality standards.
• The growth in exports is crucial for achieving the target of doubling farmers' income and strengthening India's position in global food trade.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: APEDA (Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority) promotes agricultural exports.
The 'One District One Product' (ODOP) scheme aims to promote indigenous products.
India exports a variety of agricultural products including rice, spices, and marine products.
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors contributing to the growth in India's agricultural exports and its impact on farmer welfare.
Analyze the challenges faced by Indian agricultural exporters in meeting international quality standards and logistics.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies and schemes in promoting agricultural exports and value addition.
Suggest measures to further enhance agricultural export potential and integrate Indian farmers into global value chains.
GS3.imageDescription: A vibrant image of various fresh fruits and vegetables, symbolizing agricultural produce.
GS3.trivia: India is the world's largest producer of milk, pulses, and spices.

GS3.headline: Rise in Cyber Attacks on Critical Infrastructure Prompts Call for Enhanced National Cybersecurity Strategy
GS3.explainer:
• India has witnessed a concerning increase in sophisticated cyber attacks targeting critical infrastructure sectors, including power grids, financial systems, and telecommunications.
• These attacks, often state-sponsored or from organized cybercrime groups, pose significant national security and economic risks.
• Experts are calling for an urgent review and enhancement of India's National Cybersecurity Strategy and incident response mechanisms.
• Key recommendations include strengthening public-private partnerships, investing in advanced cyber defense technologies, and building a skilled cybersecurity workforce.
• The government is reportedly considering new legislative measures to mandate higher security standards for critical infrastructure operators.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Critical Infrastructure includes power grids, financial systems, and telecommunications.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the nodal agency for cybersecurity incidents.
Cyber attacks pose threats to national security and economy.
Need for stronger public-private partnerships in cybersecurity.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cyber threats to India's critical infrastructure and their potential impact on national security and economic stability.
Analyze the strengths and weaknesses of India's current cybersecurity framework and strategy in protecting vital digital assets.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships and international cooperation in building a resilient cybersecurity ecosystem.
Suggest comprehensive measures, including legislative, technological, and human resource development, to enhance India's cybersecurity capabilities.
GS3.imageDescription: A digital representation of interconnected networks with a lock icon, symbolizing cybersecurity.
GS3.trivia: India ranks among the top countries targeted by cyberattacks globally.

GS3.headline: India Aims for Accelerated Solar Energy Capacity Expansion, Boosts Manufacturing
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced ambitious targets for solar energy capacity expansion, aiming for 500 GW by 2030.
• New policies and incentives are being rolled out to boost domestic manufacturing of solar cells, modules, and related equipment.
• The focus is on reducing reliance on imports, fostering self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat) in the solar sector, and creating jobs.
• Challenges include land acquisition, grid integration, and ensuring stable financing for large-scale projects.
• The expansion is critical for achieving India's climate goals and ensuring energy security in the long run.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: India's target for renewable energy capacity is 500 GW by 2030.
Focus on domestic manufacturing of solar cells and modules.
Atmanirbhar Bharat initiative promotes self-reliance.
Ministry of New and Renewable Energy oversees these initiatives.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's ambitious targets for solar energy expansion and their significance for climate action and energy security.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in boosting domestic manufacturing of solar equipment under the Atmanirbhar Bharat initiative.
Evaluate the economic and environmental benefits of transitioning to a greater share of renewable energy in India's power mix.
Suggest policy interventions and technological advancements required to overcome barriers to large-scale solar energy deployment.
GS3.imageDescription: A vast solar power plant with numerous solar panels under a clear sky.
GS3.trivia: India's Bhadla Solar Park in Rajasthan is one of the largest solar parks in the world.
